stories.headline: [EurAsian Times] Nearly 50% of Iran's Missile Launchers, Kamikaze Drones Intact, Intel Claims as Tehran Vows "Crushing Response"
stories.summary: Iran has vowed a "crushing response" to US and Israeli strikes, with nearly 50% of its missile launchers and kamikaze drones still intact, according to a new intelligence assessment. Despite recent attacks, Iran's military capabilities remain significant, with sources warning they can still "wreak absolute havoc" in the region. The assessment suggests that half of Iran's missile launchers are still operational. Iran has launched missile attacks across the Middle East, targeting US-linked assets, energy infrastructure, and civilian areas. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been relocated to a secure location. The conflict between Iran and its allies, and the US and Israel, shows no signs of easing, with Tehran preparing for retaliation and warning of a "crushing" response to the joint daylight strike. The situation remains volatile, with ongoing exchanges of fire.

stories.headline: [The Guardian] ‘Food security timebomb’: a visual guide to the Gulf fertiliser blockade UN says record numbers of people could face acute hunger if conflict continues
stories.summary: The UN warns that record numbers of people could face acute hunger if the conflict in the Persian Gulf continues, due to a blockade on fertiliser shipments. The World Trade Organization considers fertilisers the top concern, with roughly a third of global raw fertiliser materials and 20% of natural gas shipments used to make fertiliser transiting the Strait of Hormuz. In 2024, about 16m tonnes of fertilisers were moved by sea from the Gulf. The Qatar Fertiliser Company, which supplies around 14% of global fertiliser, is affected. The UN Food and Agriculture Organization's Chief Economist describes the situation as one of the most rapid and severe disruptions to global commodity flows in recent times, potentially leading to food inflation and acute hunger for millions of people. The conflict is exacerbating global food security concerns.

stories.headline: [Mint] US-Iran war: Trump threatens escalation amid reported talks - ‘bridges next, then electric powerplants’
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to escalate the US-Iran war, targeting Iran's bridges and energy infrastructure. Despite reported talks between Washington and Tehran, Trump stated that the US military "hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran" and warned that bridges and electric power plants would be next. He also threatened strikes against Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island. Tehran has denied direct talks and suggested Trump's threats are a ploy to reduce energy prices and buy time for military plans. The Iranian military has warned of retaliatory attacks if the US proceeds with its threats. Trump claims "great progress" has been made in negotiations, but his comments suggest a potential escalation of the conflict, which has been ongoing for five weeks. The situation remains tense, with both sides exchanging threats and warnings.

stories.headline: [NDTV] US Allies Work On Plan B For Hormuz Strait If Trump Walks Away
stories.summary: US allies are working on a backup plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz in case President Donald Trump's administration fails to resolve the crisis. Over 40 allies met to discuss diplomatic outreach and potential sanctions on Iran. The UK led the talks, driven by concerns that the US may end its conflict with Iran without resolving the Hormuz crisis. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipment route, has been impacted by the US-Israel conflict with Iran. President Trump has called for a naval coalition to secure the strait, but has expressed pessimism about allies' willingness to help. Despite this, the US is working to assemble a coalition to reopen the strait, with a focus on building political commitment from allies. Retailers have warned of potential 9% food inflation due to the crisis.

stories.headline: Living Brain Cells Enable Machine Learning Computations A research team at Tohoku University and Future University Hakodate has demonstrated that living biological neurons can be trained to perform a
stories.summary: A research team at Tohoku University and Future University Hakodate has demonstrated that living biological neurons can be trained to perform a supervised temporal pattern learning task. This task was previously carried out by artificial systems, but the team showed that biological systems can generate complex time-series signals. By integrating cultured neuronal networks into a machine learning framework, the team enabled living brain cells to perform machine learning computations. The study used in vitro biological neural networks to investigate how living cells interact with their environments and shape high-dimensional dynamics. The results show that living brain cells can be used to generate coherent temporal outputs, such as those required for motor control, and can be trained to perform tasks previously done by artificial systems. This breakthrough has potential implications for the development of new machine learning technologies.

stories.headline: ASUS brings Ceraluminum to their 2026 Zenbook range alongside a new dual-display Zenbook Duo ASUS debuts Ceraluminum—a blend of ceramic and aluminum—across the Zenbook range for a premium, durable, and ultra-lightweight mobile experience.
stories.summary: ASUS is introducing Ceraluminum, a blend of ceramic and aluminum, across its 2026 Zenbook range for a premium, durable, and ultra-lightweight experience. The new material will be used in the Zenbook S14, S16, and the dual-screen Zenbook Duo. The 2026 Zenbook Duo features a redesigned hide-away hinge, a new chassis made with Ceraluminum, and dual upgraded ASUS Lumina Pro OLED displays. Pre-orders for the Zenbook Duo (2026) UX8407 are now open, with shipments expected to start on April 8, 2026. The laptop is built for professionals and creators, offering more power, greater portability, and longer battery life. The Ceraluminum chassis provides exceptional durability while maintaining a lightweight form factor, making it suitable for demanding users.

stories.headline: Man raves about 'hidden' device in car that's revolutionising how Aussies travel: 'Game-changer' The revolutionary device is helping those on the road stay connected.
stories.summary: A man has discovered a "hidden" device in his car that is revolutionizing the way Australians travel. The device is helping people stay connected while on the road. This comes as the Australian government has announced a reduction in the heavy vehicle road user charge to zero, aiming to assist truck drivers. The development is part of the latest car news in Australia, which includes new vehicle releases, industry updates, and sales figures. The discovery of the device has been described as a "game-changer" for those who spend a lot of time on the road, enhancing their travel experience. The news is significant for Australians who rely on their vehicles for daily commutes, road trips, or work-related travel, and are looking for ways to stay connected and improve their overall travel experience.

stories.headline: Nothing Phone (4a) Review: Transparent Style Meets Serious Specs on a Budget The Nothing Phone (4a) series was announced last month, Trevor has just checked out the Nothing Phone (4a) Pro and it’s time to get down with the more
stories.summary: The Nothing Phone (4a) series was recently announced, featuring a 6.78" display with a sharper 1.5K resolution and increased brightness of 1600 nits. The display is slightly larger than the previous model. A review of the Nothing Phone (4a) Pro has been conducted, highlighting its transparent style and serious specs at a budget-friendly price. The phone's features and performance are being evaluated, with the display being a key aspect of the device. The Nothing Phone (4a) series aims to provide a reliable and high-quality smartphone experience without breaking the bank. With its improved display and specs, the phone is expected to be a competitive option in the market. The review of the Nothing Phone (4a) Pro provides an in-depth look at the device's capabilities and value.

stories.headline: Final teams in as under-fire Blues face Roos in Good Friday opener.
stories.summary: Carlton has defeated North Melbourne by 82 points in their Good Friday AFL clash, with a final score of 24.9 (153) to 11.5 (71). The Blues made four changes to their team, while the Roos recalled veterans Jack Darling and Zac Banch to replace suspended ruck Tristan Xerri and rested youngster Lachy Dovaston. Carlton's Jacob Weitering and Brodie Kemp were named as substitute backs. The win marks a significant turnaround for the Blues, who have struggled in the second half of games this season. The team's top performers included Hewett with 34 disposals, Cerra with 28, and Walsh with 22. The victory gives Carlton a much-needed boost, while North Melbourne will look to regroup after the convincing loss. The match was part of a crucial round four of AFL battles.

stories.headline: Anthony Edwards misses eligibility mark for season-ending NBA awards
stories.summary: Anthony Edwards, a player for the Minnesota Timberwolves, has been ruled out for the remainder of the season, making him ineligible for the NBA's season-ending awards. Edwards missed the team's 113-108 loss to the Detroit Pistons due to injury, which put him below the 65-game minimum required for award eligibility. As a result, he will not be considered for All-NBA honors. Edwards' absence from the awards landscape is due to the NBA's rule requiring players to participate in at least 65 games to be eligible. His inability to meet this mark will have tangible consequences, ultimately costing him a chance at All-NBA recognition despite his performance during the games he played. Edwards' injury has resulted in him missing a crucial number of games, falling short of the required threshold.

stories.headline: Abhishek Sharma fined by IPL after run-out controversy in KKR vs SRH
stories.summary: Abhishek Sharma has been fined 25 percent of his match fees and handed one demerit point for breaching the IPL's Code of Conduct during the SRH vs KKR match at Eden Gardens on April 2. The match referee found him guilty of violating Article 2.3, which deals with the use of offensive, obscene, or profane language. The incident occurred despite SRH's dominant performance against KKR. Sharma's sanction was confirmed by the Indian Premier League, resulting in the fine and demerit point. The all-rounder's conduct during the game led to the penalty, which is in accordance with the IPL's Code of Conduct. The fine and demerit point will be taken into account in any future disciplinary actions against Sharma. The incident has drawn attention to the importance of adhering to the IPL's Code of Conduct.

stories.headline: LSG Share "True Story" Behind Sanjiv Goenka-Rishabh Pant Controversy Amid Social Media Debate
stories.summary: Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) shared a post-match video to clarify the controversy surrounding team owner Sanjiv Goenka and captain Rishabh Pant. The video was posted in response to widespread debate on social media, triggered by earlier videos that appeared to show tension between Goenka and Pant. LSG's video, captioned "Not everything you see is the true story," showed the two individuals talking and eventually hugging, dispelling rumors of a rift. The team aimed to provide an unfiltered look at the post-match atmosphere, suggesting that the initial videos did not tell the full story. The controversy had sparked a heated online debate, with many speculating about the nature of the interaction between Goenka and Pant. LSG's video attempted to set the record straight, showcasing a more relaxed and amicable exchange between the two.

stories.headline: Techie dies by suicide in Hyderabad a month after marriage over dowry dispute
stories.summary: A 26-year-old software engineer, Ishika Yadav, died by suicide in her apartment in Hyderabad's Miyapur area. The incident occurred just over a month after her marriage to Neeraj Bansal, also a software professional. Yadav, a native of Bihar, was found hanging from a ceiling fan in her apartment. Police are probing allegations of dowry harassment against her husband. The couple had gotten married earlier this year, and authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding Yadav's death. The incident highlights the ongoing issue of dowry-related harassment in India, which can have devastating consequences. Police are currently investigating the case and have not yet released further details. The incident has sparked concern and outrage in the community, with many calling for greater awareness and action to prevent such tragedies.

stories.headline: Pakistan Defence Minister Khawaja Asif on Rajnath Singh's remarks: 'Response to any attack would be 'swift, calibrated and decisive'
stories.summary: Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif warned that any attack from India would be met with a "swift, calibrated, and decisive" response. Asif's statement came in response to Indian Defence Minister Rajnath Singh's comments, cautioning Pakistan against any "misadventure" amid rising tensions. Asif accused India of "strategic anxiety" and stated that any future confrontation would see a more forceful Pakistani retaliation. The exchange of warnings comes ahead of the anniversary of the Pahalgam attack. Asif took to social media to respond to Singh's remarks, emphasizing that Pakistan is prepared to respond to any potential attack. The statements reflect escalating tensions between the two nations, with both defence ministers issuing warnings to each other. The situation remains volatile, with Pakistan and India engaging in a war of words.

stories.headline: INS Taragiri, with BrahMos on board, to be inducted in Navy today
stories.summary: INS Taragiri, an indigenously built stealth frigate equipped with BrahMos missiles, is being inducted into the Indian Navy. The commissioning ceremony, presided over by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, took place in Visakhapatnam. This induction marks a significant boost to the Navy's combat capability. The INS Taragiri is a testament to India's advanced design and engineering in naval technology. The ship's addition to the fleet is expected to enhance the Navy's firepower and capabilities. The induction is part of the Indian Navy's efforts to modernize and strengthen its fleet, with another warship, INS Aridhaman, also being inducted recently. The INS Taragiri's commissioning is a major milestone in the Indian Navy's expansion and upgrading of its capabilities.

stories.headline: 33 days, no answers: Family of India’s first West Asia war casualty moves Bombay HC to bring sailor’s body home
stories.summary: The family of Dixit Solanki, India's first casualty of the West Asia war, has moved the Bombay High Court to bring his body home. Solanki, a sailor, was on duty on March 1 when his vessel was hit by a missile, resulting in an explosion and engine room breach. He was initially reported missing but later located near the hull breach site. Despite 33 days passing, the family has received no clarity from authorities on when they can lay him to rest. Frustrated by the delay, they have sought urgent intervention from the court, which is likely to hear the matter next week. The family is seeking to bring Solanki's body home for a proper burial, but so far, there has been no timeline provided by the authorities.

stories.headline: Geopolitics of the Third World War Examine how the Third World War (geopolitics) reflects a radical shift from traditional world politics to a new multipolar order.
stories.summary: The Third World War is being fought between those seeking to preserve the collective West's hegemony and multipolar humanity. The US initiated this conflict to maintain its unipolar model of world order. The war reflects a radical shift from traditional world politics to a new multipolar order. Four critical dimensions - social, economic, political, and military - are being examined to understand the patterns that led to World War I and II. The conflict is a struggle for global power, with the West trying to maintain its dominance and multipolar nations seeking to establish a new order. A shift in world order usually occurs through war, as those in power rarely relinquish it voluntarily, holding on until they are destroyed. The ongoing war is a manifestation of this struggle for global dominance and a new world order.

stories.headline: Power, petroleum and policy in focus at Future Career Conversations Explore insights on energy security, geopolitics, and India's strategic response at the Future Career Conversations panel discussion.
stories.summary: India's energy security is under scrutiny due to its reliance on imported oil, with nearly 85% of its oil being imported. The Ukraine war has disrupted global energy flows, intensifying vulnerabilities. A panel discussion at Future Career Conversations explored the strategic shift in control over energy resources and its impact on global influence. The US, a major producer of petroleum and LNG, is repositioning itself to exert control over energy markets and the global financial system. India faces strategic repercussions, including diplomatic and economic implications, with global oil prices crossing $100 per barrel. Efforts to diversify supply are underway, including agreements to import LPG from the US, which will account for roughly 10% of India's demand. The discussion highlighted the need for policymakers to adapt energy strategies to volatility in global markets.

stories.headline: ‘India is 100% import‑dependent for Helium, making many industries acutely vulnerable to global disruptions’
stories.summary: India is 100% import-dependent on helium, with an estimated demand of 3.4 million cubic meters in 2025. Over 50% of India's helium imports come from Qatar, making the country vulnerable to supply disruptions. The recent attack on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG hub has raised concerns over helium availability, a critical component in semiconductor manufacturing. India maintains only 7-10 days of inventory, making it highly susceptible to supply shocks. This vulnerability poses a significant risk to India's semiconductor and electronics industry, which is crucial for the country's ambitions to become a global manufacturing hub. The industry relies heavily on helium, and any disruption in supply can have immediate and serious consequences, highlighting the need to treat critical gases like helium as strategic inputs.

stories.headline: Silverline Technologies Pivots to Cyber Warfare Amid User Surge Silverline Technologies announces a temporary closure of its digital platform for maintenance following a sudden 45% increase in daily active users.
stories.summary: Silverline Technologies has announced a 24-72 hour temporary closure of its digital platform for maintenance due to a 45% surge in daily active users. The company will use this time to upgrade its infrastructure and enhance platform capacity and performance. Additionally, Silverline Technologies is pivoting its AI division to focus on high-demand areas such as cyber warfare, supply chain management, and energy sectors. This strategic move is in response to global geopolitics and macroeconomic trends. The company aims to develop advanced technologies in these areas, positioning itself for growth amidst India's AI surge. The pivot is part of Silverline Technologies' efforts to reinvent itself as a legacy IT firm and capitalize on emerging technological shifts. The maintenance and upgrade are expected to be completed within 24-72 hours, after which the platform will resume operations.

stories.headline: Kapil Sibal represents Mamata govt in SC after mob violence in Muslim-dominated Malda: Read about his past record
stories.summary: Kapil Sibal, a senior Indian lawyer and politician, is representing the Mamata Banerjee government in the Supreme Court. This comes after mob violence occurred in Malda, a Muslim-dominated area. Sibal, born on August 8, 1948, has served as a Union Minister in the Manmohan Singh government and as President of the Supreme Court Bar Association. He has been involved in several high-profile cases, including the Ram Janmabhoomi-Babri Masjid title dispute and the Shayara Bano vs Union of India case. Sibal has also been indicted by the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) for favoring a company in a database creation project. He is a prominent member of the Group of 23 leaders who demanded a party overhaul and visible leadership from Sonia Gandhi. Sibal's representation of the West Bengal government is part of an intense legal battle at the Supreme Court.

stories.headline: Biden Administration Faces Backlash Over Handling of Classified Documents
stories.summary: The Biden Administration is facing backlash over its handling of classified documents. Recent investigations have led to the FBI secretly seeking records of Trump allies, including Corey Lewandowski and Dan Scavino. Meanwhile, President Trump has revoked former President Biden's access to classified information and daily intelligence briefings. The issue has sparked criticism, with some lawmakers expressing concern over the administration's transparency and accountability. The controversy is part of a larger debate over the handling of sensitive information and the role of government officials in investigations. The administration's response to these issues has been met with skepticism, fueling further criticism and calls for greater transparency. The situation remains ongoing, with new developments emerging as the administration navigates the complex issue of classified documents.

stories.headline: US Senate Votes to Block New Immigration Rule
stories.summary: The US Senate voted to block a new immigration rule, amidst a prolonged shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). The shutdown, which lasted 35 days, was due to disagreements over funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Senate Democrats voted to block a House-passed bill to fund ICE, while House Republicans rejected a Senate-passed bill that would finance most of the DHS but withhold funds from ICE. After negotiations, the Senate agreed to a modified bill that would fund the department except for ICE and immigration enforcement operations. The Senate ultimately voted unanimously to reopen the DHS, but without funding for immigration enforcement and deportation operations, in an effort to end the 40-day shutdown. The vote reflects ongoing disagreements between Democrats and Republicans over immigration policy and funding.

stories.headline: European Union Leaders Meet to Discuss Rising Tensions with Russia
stories.summary: European Union leaders met in Brussels to discuss rising tensions with Russia over Ukraine. The meeting included counterparts from Ukraine and four other former Soviet republics, with a focus on deepening political, trade, energy, and cultural ties. The EU aims to reduce dependence on Russia and other foreign powers, with plans to boost offshore wind power in the North Sea. Except for Hungary, the leaders are expected to show strong support for Kyiv as the Russia-Ukraine war enters its fifth year. The summit also included discussions with European Parliament President Roberta Metsola and a visit from UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres. The meeting comes as Russia continues to remove troops, and the EU seeks to strengthen its defense capabilities and address economic and political impacts of recent global developments. The EU leaders' meeting aims to demonstrate unity and solidarity with Ukraine.

stories.headline: Good Friday 2026: Is stock market open or closed today? Stock markets observe Good Friday holiday, resume trading Monday
stories.summary: The United States stock markets, including the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and Nasdaq, are closed on Good Friday, April 3, 2026, in observance of the holiday commemorating the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ. Although Good Friday is not a federally recognized holiday, the stock markets traditionally close on this day. The markets will remain closed through the weekend and reopen on Monday, April 6, with regular trading hours resuming at 9:30 a.m. ET. The NYSE and Nasdaq will close at 4 p.m. ET on Monday. This closure is a standard practice, despite Good Friday not being one of the 12 federal holidays recognized in 2026.

stories.headline: Sensex Rallies Over 200 Points, Nifty Reclaims 17,200 Amid Global Cues
stories.summary: The Sensex rallied over 200 points, with the Nifty reclaiming 17,200, amid positive global cues. The 30-share index surged 477.24 points to end at 57,897.48, a 0.83% increase. The Nifty rose by 147.20 points to 17,233.45, a 0.86% increase. Index heavyweights such as Reliance Industries, Infosys, and HDFC Bank drove the gains. Similarly, on another day, the Sensex ended 113 points higher, and the Nifty rose 27 points to 17,248.40, tracking a positive trend overseas. Global markets also showed an upward trend, with the S&P 500 ending higher and the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising 153.3 points. However, in recent trades, the Sensex tanked over 1,200 points, and the Nifty 50 fell below 23,000, with stocks like L&T and RIL being heavily traded.

stories.headline: Indian Stock Market Ends Higher, Led By Gains In IT And Pharma Stocks
stories.summary: The Indian stock market ended higher, led by gains in IT and pharma stocks. The Sensex rose 185 points to close at 73,320, and the Nifty gained 34 points to settle at 22,713. Earlier, the indices had staged a gap down opening after US President Donald Trump hinted at prolonging the war in Iran. However, they rebounded more than 2% from the day's lows, supported by buying in IT and banking stocks. The market recovery was also driven by a ceasefire between India and Pakistan and a breakthrough US-China trade deal, sparking a rally on Dalal Street. IT, metals, and travel stocks led the surge amid broad-based buying, with the Sensex closing 320.70 points higher at 81,633.02 and the Nifty50 settling 81.15 points higher at 24,833.60. The broader market indices outperformed, with strong buying interest seen across largecap, midcap, and smallcap stocks.

stories.headline: BSE Sensex Surges 500 Points, Nifty Above 17,500 As Markets Recover From Early Losses
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex surged 500 points, with the Nifty exceeding 17,500, as markets recovered from early losses. This rebound follows a volatile trading period, where the Sensex had fallen 5,252 points over six sessions since September 27. Previously, the market had shown significant fluctuations, including a 463-point gain on the last trading day and a 960-point surge two days ago, which was later pulled back by 450 points due to profit-taking. The Nifty 50 also recovered from its day's lows, gaining 42.95 points. The recent movements indicate a highly unpredictable market, with significant daily swings in both the Sensex and Nifty indices. Despite the early losses, the market's ability to roar back into the green territory suggests ongoing investor activity and potential for further volatility.

stories.headline: Microsoft to invest $10 billion in Japan for AI and cyber defence expansion
stories.summary: Microsoft will invest $10 billion (approximately ¥1.6 trillion) in Japan from 2026 to 2029. The investment is focused on three pillars: Technology, Trust, and Talent. It aims to expand artificial intelligence infrastructure, strengthen cybersecurity cooperation with the government, and deepen public-private partnerships. The company plans to collaborate with domestic partners to expand AI infrastructure options within Japan and invest in its own in-country infrastructure. This initiative supports Japan's technological growth and national security. The investment will be made over a four-year period, with the goal of improving Japan's artificial intelligence environment and enhancing its cybersecurity capabilities. Microsoft's investment is a significant commitment to Japan's technological development and national security.

stories.headline: ICICI CEO sees India in a ‘Goldilocks moment’ amid strong stability and rising global confidence
stories.summary: ICICI Bank CEO Sandeep Bakhshi stated that India is in a "Goldilocks moment" with strong economic growth, macroeconomic stability, and rising global confidence. This phase is characterized by a balance of economic growth, manageable inflation, and sustained expansion. Bakhshi highlighted this at IIM Jammu's convocation, where he also emphasized the transformative role of digital infrastructure in financial inclusion. He noted that India's strong growth is driven by human capital and digital progress. Bakhshi also spoke at XLRI's convocation, urging graduates to learn continuously and lead with purpose. He emphasized that India's growth is fueled by people and technology, and stressed the importance of global awareness and human skills. India's current economic state is seen as a rare phase of equilibrium, distinct from the struggles of other global economies.

stories.headline: Pound Sterling edges higher as traders price in two BoE rate hike odds
stories.summary: The Pound Sterling edged higher as traders priced in the possibility of two Bank of England (BoE) rate hikes in 2026. The British pound rose above $1.33 after the BoE held rates at 3.75% with a unanimous vote, adopting a more hawkish tone than expected. Policymakers warned that the Middle East conflict could push near-term inflation higher, driving two-year UK bond yields to a 14-month high. The GBP/USD gained as markets weighed the risks of rising inflation and awaited further policy decisions. Investors expect rates to stay at 3.75% with one possible hike by year-end, amid fading rate-cut bets. The UK economy's avoidance of outright stagnation supports the view that the BoE may delay further interest rate cuts until later in 2025. The US Dollar struggled to advance as traders awaited speeches from Federal Reserve policymakers.

stories.headline: IFC, CycleFlow partner on access to finance for MSMEs
stories.summary: The International Finance Corporation (IFC) and CycleFlow have partnered to provide access to finance for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in Nigeria. CycleFlow, a Nigeria-based working capital and supply chain finance partner, is powered by C2FO, a global on-demand working capital platform. The partnership has launched a supply chain finance platform in Nigeria, aiming to transform access to finance for Nigerian MSMEs. This move is the first phase of a comprehensive nationwide working capital platform strategy for Africa and other emerging markets. With support from various banking partners, the platform has already secured multiple multinational and local customers, marking a significant step in improving financial access for MSMEs in the region. The partnership aims to address the working capital needs of MSMEs, promoting economic growth and development in Nigeria.

stories.headline: The AGI Awakening: AI’s accelerated evolution and the dawn of a new era Artificial intelligence (AI) has shifted from narrow task-based systems to reasoning, creative and self-improving intelligence at a double-exponential pace, wit
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) has rapidly evolved from narrow task-based systems to reasoning, creative, and self-improving intelligence at a double-exponential pace. Recent models like GPT-4 and Claude 3.5 Sonnet are nearing human-like cognition, demonstrating capabilities such as reasoning and creativity. This progress has accelerated the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI), with some predicting its emergence as early as 2026 or 2027. Governments and Big Tech companies, including OpenAI, are leading the development of AI, while also suppressing open-source alternatives and implementing censorship. The rapid advancement of AI has sparked speculation about the potential emergence of artificial superintelligence (ASI) in the near future, with many experts weighing in on the topic and its potential implications. The development of AGI and ASI is expected to have significant impacts on various domains, including math, coding, and more.

stories.headline: Microsoft has launched a suite of its own artificial intelligence (AI) models as the company goes head-to-head with offerings from OpenAI, Google and Anthropic
stories.summary: Microsoft has launched a suite of its own artificial intelligence (AI) models, including MAI-Voice-1 and a preview version of MAI-1, for transcription, voice, and image generation. The models are available on Foundry, a platform that allows developers to access and integrate AI capabilities into their applications. This move marks a significant shift for Microsoft, as it reduces its reliance on OpenAI and positions itself to compete with other major AI players, including Google and Anthropic. The launch of Microsoft's in-house AI models raises questions about the company's ongoing partnership with OpenAI. The new models are expected to offer lower-cost alternatives to existing AI solutions, potentially disrupting the market and changing the dynamics of the AI landscape. Microsoft's move is seen as a strategic effort to expand its AI capabilities and offerings.

stories.headline: Microsoft Corp. aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by next year, part of a push to build in-house alternatives to the most power
stories.summary: Microsoft Corp. aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by next year. This effort is part of a push to build in-house alternatives to the most powerful AI tools from OpenAI and Anthropic. The company is working to create its own advanced AI models, reducing reliance on external tools. Microsoft's goal is to have these large-scale AI models developed and ready by next year, as part of its efforts to enhance its AI capabilities. The development of in-house AI models will allow Microsoft to better compete with other tech companies and provide more innovative solutions to its customers. This move is seen as a strategic step for Microsoft to strengthen its position in the AI market and reduce dependence on third-party AI tools.

stories.headline: There are only 3 headlines provided, therefore only these 3 can be returned.
stories.summary: Negotiations between Afghanistan and Pakistan are advancing, according to China, as the two countries meet to try to end their worst conflict since the Taliban returned to power in 2021. Meanwhile, the COVID-19 variant BA.3.2, or "Cicada," has been detected in at least 23 countries and half of the US states. In other news, researchers have made a breakthrough in understanding why some people, estimated to be between 5-35%, do not get sick from the flu despite exposure to the virus. A team of researchers analyzed mouthwash samples from 19 healthcare workers who reported never getting flu symptoms, despite frequent contact with infected patients, in an effort to determine the reason behind their immunity. Additionally, issues with healthcare access for kids in foster care have been reported, with many doctors not accepting patients on their insurance plans.
